PreToolUse.description: Block component duplication — enforce global component reuse
PreToolUse.prompt:
A file is about to be written.

File: {{tool_input.file_path}}

Rules:
1. If this file is inside a page/route directory AND defines a reusable React component (button, card, form, modal, list, nav, table, input), BLOCK IT. Components must go in src/components/.
2. If the component name matches something in the project's component registry, suggest the existing one.
3. Files in src/components/ are fine — those ARE global components.
4. Page compositions (importing and arranging components) are fine.
5. Plain HTML files, CSS files, JS files — always fine.
6. Config, utility, hook, test files — always fine.

If violation: exit 2 with reason.
If fine: exit 0.

PreToolUse.description: Block hardcoded secrets in any file
PreToolUse.prompt:
A file is about to be written.

File: {{tool_input.file_path}}

Check for:
1. API keys (sk_live_*, pk_live_*, AKIA*, ghp_*, etc.)
2. Hardcoded passwords
3. Database URLs with real credentials
4. Private keys or tokens

Ignore:
- .env.example (placeholder values)
- Test files with fake values
- process.env.* or import.meta.env.* references
- Documentation files

If secrets found: exit 2.
If clean: exit 0.

A bash command is about to run.

Command: {{tool_input.command}}

Block (exit 2) if:
1. Deletes outside project dir (rm -rf /*, rm -rf ~/*)
2. Force pushes to main/master
3. Drops database tables without migration context
4. Modifies system files
5. Installs globally without user request

Allow (exit 0) if:
- Standard dev commands (npm, npx, node, git, mkdir, etc.)
- Within project directory
- Creates/modifies project files

If blocked: explain what's dangerous.

PreToolUse.description: Block removal of security-critical code patterns
PreToolUse.prompt:
A file edit is about to happen.

File: {{tool_input.file_path}}
Removing: {{tool_input.old_string}}
Adding: {{tool_input.new_string}}

Block (exit 2) if the edit:
1. Removes try/catch error handling without replacement
2. Removes auth/authorization checks
3. Removes input validation
4. Replaces parameterized queries with string concatenation
5. Removes CORS or security headers

Allow (exit 0) if:
- Refactoring (replacing with equivalent or better code)
- Removing truly unused code
- Normal development edits

If blocked: explain the security concern.

PreToolUse.description: Remind agents to use the index instead of scanning files

PostToolUse.description: Auto-format newly created files with Prettier
Remind agent to update the project index after file creation
Check npm install output for vulnerabilities

Stop.description: Verify agent completed all tasks and updated the index
Stop.prompt:
The agent is about to stop.

Verify:
1. Were all assigned tasks completed? Check if agent mentioned completing tasks.
2. Did the agent update .10x/file-index.json with new files?
3. Did the agent log work in .10x/dev-log.md?
4. Did the agent report results to the user?
5. Were any errors left unresolved?

If everything looks good: exit 0.
If critical work is incomplete (tasks not done, index not updated): exit 2 with what's missing.
If only minor things missing (dev-log not updated): exit 0 with reminder.

SessionStart.description: Detect project state and suggest next action
SessionStart.command: if [ -f '.10x/project.json' ]; then echo '{"status": "project_exists", "message": "Existing 10x project found. Use /10x-development-team:resume to continue."}'; cat .10x/project.json 2>/dev/null | head -5; elif [ -f 'package.json' ]; then echo '{"status": "non_10x_project", "message": "Project found but not initialized with 10x. Use /10x-development-team:start to set up."}'; else echo '{"status": "no_project", "message": "No project found. Use /10x-development-team:start to begin."}'; fi

SubagentStart.description: Ensure subagent has access to project index
SubagentStart.prompt:
A specialist agent is being spawned.

Agent: {{agent_name}}

Check the spawn prompt includes ALL of:
1. Reading .10x/project.json
2. Reading .10x/file-index.json
3. Reading .10x/feature-map.json
4. Reading relevant .claude/knowledge/ file
5. Instruction to update file-index.json after work
6. Instruction to update feature-map.json after work
7. Instruction to append to dev-log.md
8. Clear acceptance criteria

If any are missing, output:
{"reminder": "Delegation missing: [list missing items]. Agent may produce incorrect or untracked output."}

Always exit 0.

SubagentStop.description: Verify subagent updated the project index
SubagentStop.prompt:
A specialist agent just finished.

Agent: {{agent_name}}

Check ALL of these:
1. Did the agent complete its assigned tasks?
2. Did it follow the global component registry (if frontend work)?
3. Did it update .10x/file-index.json with every file created or modified?
4. Did it update .10x/feature-map.json with feature wiring (for MVP/Production scope)?
5. Did it append to .10x/dev-log.md with what was done?
6. Did it update .10x/tasks.json status?

If any of items 3-6 are missing, output:
{"concerns": ["Agent did not update [missing file]. Index may be out of sync."]}

Always exit 0.

PreCompact.description: Save current state to index before context compaction
PreCompact.command: if [ -f '.10x/project.json' ]; then echo '{"pre_compact": "Project index exists at .10x/ — will be reloaded after compaction"}'; fi

PostCompact.description: Remind to reload project index after compaction
PostCompact.command: if [ -f '.10x/project.json' ]; then echo '{"post_compact": "Context was compacted. Re-read .10x/project.json and .10x/file-index.json before continuing work."}'; fi

SessionEnd.description: Final dev-log entry when session ends
SessionEnd.command: if [ -f '.10x/dev-log.md' ]; then echo '' >> .10x/dev-log.md && echo "## $(date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') — Session Ended" >> .10x/dev-log.md && echo '- **Agent:** system' >> .10x/dev-log.md && echo '- **Action:** Session closed' >> .10x/dev-log.md && echo '---' >> .10x/dev-log.md; fi 2>/dev/null || true
